main.description: The foundational record. Everything else references it. A world must exist before characters, events, or logs can exist inside it.

creatorDID.description: The permanent identity of the world's originator

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this world was created

description.description: What kind of world this is

sourceType.description: The intellectual property status of this world

sourceReference.description: If sourceType is publicDomain, the specific source material

governanceMode.description: The constitutional posture of the world — sandbox (anything goes), governed (originator rules), or constitutional (community governance)

canonicalStatus.description: The canonical standing of this world

renderingHints.description: Metadata that tells rendering clients what kind of world this is

tone.description: The emotional register of the world (e.g., dark, whimsical, gritty)

era.description: The time period or setting (e.g., Victorian, far future, mythic)

genre.description: The genre classification (e.g., fantasy, noir, romance, publication)

aesthetic.description: Visual or stylistic direction (e.g., minimalist, baroque, wasteland sparse)
